title: pilosella lactucella subsp. littuanica
intro: Pilosella lactucella subsp. littuanica is a rare and little-studied plant species, which is a subspecies of the Pilosella lactucella plant, a type of dandelion-like flowering plant in the sunflower family. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Lithuania, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Pilosella lactucella subsp. littuanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its safety and efficacy as a medicinal plant.
